title: Arrogance and Pride
subtitle: The First Sin of Iblis — The Quality That Bars Entry into Paradise

verses.arabic: وَلَا تُصَعِّرْ خَدَّكَ لِلنَّاسِ وَلَا تَمْشِ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَحًا ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتَالٍ فَخُورٍ
verses.translation: And do not turn your cheek [in contempt] toward people and do not walk through the earth exultantly. Indeed, Allah does not like everyone self-deluded and boastful.

verses.arabic: قَالَ فَاهْبِطْ مِنْهَا فَمَا يَكُونُ لَكَ أَن تَتَكَبَّرَ فِيهَا فَاخْرُجْ إِنَّكَ مِنَ الصَّاغِرِينَ
verses.translation: [Allah] said, 'Descend from it, for it is not for you to be arrogant therein. So get out; indeed, you are of the debased.'

verses.arabic: الَّذِينَ يُجَادِلُونَ فِي آيَاتِ اللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ سُلْطَانٍ أَتَاهُمْ ۖ كَبُرَ مَقْتًا عِندَ اللَّهِ وَعِندَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَطْبَعُ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ قَلْبِ مُتَكَبِّرٍ جَبَّارٍ
verses.translation: Those who dispute concerning the signs of Allah without any authority having come to them — greatly despised is it in the sight of Allah and in the sight of those who have believed. Thus does Allah seal over every heart [belonging to] an arrogant tyrant.

brief_explanation: Arrogance (kibr) is the primal sin — the very quality that caused Iblis to disobey Allah when commanded to prostrate to Adam, leading to his eternal curse and expulsion from the heavens. The Quran records Allah's rebuke to Iblis with the words: 'Get out; you are of the debased,' establishing that arrogance is incompatible with any station of honour before Allah. Allah seals the hearts of the arrogant (mutakabbir jabbar), cutting off their ability to receive guidance and truth. The Prophet (peace be upon him) warned that no one who has even a mustard seed's weight of kibr in their heart will enter Paradise.
capital_punishment: There is no hadd (fixed corporal punishment) for arrogance as a general sin. It is a sin of the heart that leads to other sins and earns divine punishment in the Hereafter through the sealing of the heart and exclusion from Paradise. If arrogance manifests in specific criminal acts — oppression, denial of rights, refusal of lawful authority — the relevant punishments for those specific acts apply. The prescribed remedy is spiritual: sincere tawbah, tawadu (humility), and the remembrance that human beings are created from soil and will return to it.
